summary: Validated all NEW iteration-3 business rules end-to-end (backend + frontend). 7/7 new-rule pytest tests pass and full UI flow verified: one-wallet-per-network gating, deposit warning modal + 3-attempt limit + 4th=403 + securityFlag + flag-banner with admin email, deposit ledger modal with ROI activation date per deposit, admin Security tab listing flagged users with reason, clear-flag resetting attemptsRemaining back to 3, ROI cycle skipping not-yet-activated deposits (next-day activation rule for any deposit outside 05:00-05:59 PKT). Regression suite: 37/38 pass — the 1 failure is an OLD test (test_admin_run_cycle_then_user_roi) whose assumption (immediate ROI on same-day deposits) is invalidated by the NEW activation-date rule and needs updating.

minor.issue: Old regression test test_admin_run_cycle_then_user_roi asserts totalRoi>0 after a same-day deposit + cycle, but new roiStartDate=next-day rule makes this invalid outside the 05:00-05:59 PKT window. Test needs updating, not the API.

action_items: Update /app/backend/tests/test_cavi_backend.py::TestROI::test_admin_run_cycle_then_user_roi to account for next-day ROI activation (e.g., skip the totalRoi>0 assertion unless PKT hour==5, OR backdate a deposit's roiStartDate via fixture).
critical_code_review_comments: WalletsPage.js: after the 4th (flagging) deposit attempt, the input/button correctly become disabled and the flag-banner shows — confirmed via screenshot. Good UX.
AdminPanel.js Security tab loads /admin/fraud on tab change, but initial render before the response shows the empty state — minor UX quirk (could show a loading skeleton). Not blocking.
AdminPanel.js is now ~480+ lines covering 7 tabs + 2 modals; splitting into per-tab subcomponents would aid maintainability (carry-over from iter_2).
Both run-cycle and clear-flag responses don't include the affected user/record list — only {ok:true}; returning richer responses would help client cache reconciliation.
/api/wallets/{wallet_id}/deposits computes roiActive in Python loop — fine for now, but if deposits per wallet grow large, consider Mongo aggregation.

success_rate.backend: 100% on new-rule suite (7/7); 97% on full regression (37/38, 1 stale test invalidated by new rule)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (all targeted UI flows for wallet gating, deposit confirm + flagging, ledger modal, admin security tab + clear-flag verified end-to-end)

test_credentials: Superadmin: superadmin@cavi.io / Cavi@Admin2025. Test users created at runtime with TEST_/flagtest_ prefix and pass1234.
seed_data_creation: Created multiple TEST_ users via signup, several wallets (ETH, SOL), 3+ deposits each, and triggered 1-2 security flags then cleared one. Existing flagged user 'flagtest_1781901315150' remains in DB after this iteration.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All NEW iter-3 rules pass on backend (7/7) and frontend (E2E). The only failing test in the original regression suite is test_admin_run_cycle_then_user_roi which is now stale (asserts immediate ROI on same-day deposit, but the NEW rule sets roiStartDate=next-day for any deposit outside 05:00-05:59 PKT). To re-validate, the main agent should update that single test. Note that one flagged test user (flagtest_1781901315150) remains in DB and shows up in admin Security tab.
rca of the issue: The 1 regression failure is NOT a code bug — it's a test that pre-dates the new ROI activation-date logic. Reproduction: run pytest test_cavi_backend.py::TestROI::test_admin_run_cycle_then_user_roi. Server returns roiStartDate as next day for deposits made outside 05:00-05:59 PKT, so run_roi_cycle filters them out via roiStartDate<=cycleDate. Mitigation: update the test to either backdate a deposit in MongoDB before running cycle, or skip the totalRoi>0 assertion when PKT hour != 5.
